reasoning: The MMTY series historically tracks the Effective Federal Funds Rate exactly in discrete steps (e.g., holding steady at 5.33, then dropping to 4.83, 4.58, and 4.33 in exact lockstep with the Fed's 50bps, 25bps, and 25bps rate cuts in late 2024). Therefore, MMTY will only strictly increase if the Federal Reserve officially hikes interest rates. According to CME FedWatch as of mid-March 2025, markets are currently pricing a 0% chance of a rate hike by the March FOMC meeting, placing near certainty on holding steady (with some minimal chance of a rate cut). With the Fed still functioning within an assumed easing cycle (or, at minimum, a hawkish pause), short-to-medium-term chances of a rate hike are near absolute zero, driving April and June probabilities to the minimum allowable practical floor. However, the possibility of structural inflation rebounding (forcing a delayed Fed rate hike) intrinsically scales up across broader time horizons. For September 2025 and March 2026, the probability increases accordingly, reflecting typical term-structure uncertainty around monetary policy a year out.
